title: Pork Souvlaki
subtitle: Greek grilled pork skewers with lemon and oregano
excerpt: Pork souvlaki are Greek skewers of marinated pork grilled over high heat until charred at the edges and juicy inside. The marinade is simple: olive oil, lemon, oregano, and garlic. Serve in warm pitta with tzatziki, sliced tomatoes, and onion.
sourceNotes: Souvlaki, meaning little skewer, is one of the oldest fast-food dishes in Greek culture, sold from street carts throughout Greece. The pork version is the most common, though chicken and lamb versions are equally traditional. Served in pitta with tzatziki and tomato, it is one of the most popular street foods in Greece.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Marinate the pork overnight for a deeper flavour.

content.text: Pork souvlaki are small cubes of pork marinated in olive oil, lemon, and oregano, then grilled on skewers over high heat until charred and juicy. The marinade does most of the work; the cooking is quick. A griddle pan gives a good result indoors, though a hot grill or outdoor barbecue is better.
Combine the olive oil, lemon zest and juice, crushed garlic, dried oregano, salt, and pepper in a bowl. Add the pork cubes and toss to coat. Cover and refrigerate for at least 1 hour, or overnight.
If using wooden skewers, soak them in water for at least 30 minutes before threading the pork so they don't char.
Thread the pork onto skewers, 4 to 5 pieces per skewer. Heat a griddle pan over high heat until it is very hot and starting to smoke lightly.
Cook the skewers for 3 to 4 minutes per side, turning once, until char marks appear and the pork is cooked through. The internal temperature should reach 70°C. Rest for 5 minutes.
Serve in warm pitta with sliced tomato, red onion, and tzatziki. A squeeze of lemon over the hot meat just before eating lifts everything.

items.cause: Overcooked, or too lean a cut
items.fix: Pork shoulder has enough fat to stay juicy; loin and fillet dry out faster. Don't cook beyond 70°C internal temperature. A short rest after cooking helps retain the juices.

items.symptom: The skewers burn before the pork is cooked
items.cause: Wooden skewers not soaked, or heat too high
items.fix: Soak wooden skewers for at least 30 minutes. Lower the heat slightly and rotate the skewers regularly.
The griddle must be very hot before the pork goes on. Press the pork down gently to ensure full contact with the ridges.

content.text: Souvlaki is the most widespread street food in Greece. Pitta wraps with souvlaki, tzatziki, and tomato are sold from small shops all over the country. At home it is a summer staple, made on outdoor grills when the weather allows. The dish is fast, social, and forgiving, and it benefits greatly from good-quality pork and a proper char.
